title: 2024 Trek Domane AL Era White Gen 4

a) 3 bike bags: 
i. repair bag/kit (black saddle bag)
ii. split colored orange/grey oveja negra forward top tube bag
iii. black Almsthre Stem Bag with blue waterproof interior (last fixed on stem's left side)
b) Specialized Romin Evo Pro 3D-printed saddle (black)
c) black Shimano clipless road pedals
d) bike essentially otherwise outfitted with the model's stock gear/parts
e) other/accessories missing at time of theft:
i.  Giro Aries Spherical helmet (notable for its ball-in-socket Mips design)
ii. white Giro racing gloves with fingertip cutaways

title: Lectric eBikes XP foldable
description:
White with tritone blue design and lettering. Has a black cargo rack on the back. Tires are somewhere between 20 and 24 inches. Built in battery.
Found over Christmas/winter break, December 2025. Was "abandoned" along centre Ave and was lying unlocked and unattended in the grass between the road and the bike trail on the west side of Centre. After looking around and waiting 45 minutes or so, I decided to move it against the fence and put a note on it. The note basically called for the attention of the owner to claim the bike or risk forfeiture. I walked by the bike daily for 5 days, making sure it was still visible from the road and trail and that my note with phone number were still attached. On day 5, I was so excited that it was still there and it was like a Christmas present from the universe!!
Although the students aren't all back from their vacations, im pretty sure that it is still abandoned or unclaimed as there is no CSU registration sticker on it so must have had a non-student owner.
Anyway, it is now day 7 and I have brought the bike home to register and officially adopt !! :) yay

title: GT Bicycles Transeo 4.0
description:
The GT Transeo 4.0 is a hybrid bike designed for riders who want something versatile — comfortable for commuting and fitness rides, but also capable of handling light trails and gravel. Here are the main details about it:
Overview
Category: Hybrid / Fitness / Commuter
Brand: GT Bicycles 
Frame: Aluminum (lightweight, durable, upright geometry)
Fork: Equipped with a front suspension fork with a lockout option for efficient road riding
Wheels: 700c wheels (like a road bike, but wider tires for stability and comfort)
Speeds/Drivetrain: Typically a 3x8 (24-speed) Shimano or microSHIFT drivetrain
Brakes: Mechanical disc brakes (reliable stopping power in all weather, but not hydraulic)
Tires: 700x40c (good balance of speed and shock absorption)
Other features: Rack and fender mounts, blue MEC carrying panniers.

title: Supercycle BREEZE
description:
Color: Dark blue frame with a glossy finish. The paint shows minor scuffs and wear, particularly near the bottom bracket and chainstay, reflecting typical usage.

Frame Size & Design: The bike features a medium-sized frame with a slightly slanted top tube. The tube joints are visibly welded and reinforced, providing extra durability for off-road or urban riding.

Gears & Drivetrain: Equipped with a standard multi-speed gear setup. It has a triple chainring in the front and a 7-speed cassette in the rear, allowing for smooth shifting over a range of terrains. The derailleurs and shifters are operational but show minor signs of use.

Brakes: The bike uses traditional rim brakes, both front and rear, which are in good working order. The brake levers on the handlebars are black and slightly worn from use.

Handlebars: The handlebars are flat, straight, and black, with wear visible on the grips. The right grip end has a small cap missing, exposing the inner handlebar tube. The handlebar stem shows slight rust and wear, while the cables for the brakes and shifters are externally routed, curving smoothly toward the frame.

Tires & Wheels: The tires are thick, knobby, and designed for multi-surface riding, with a deep tread pattern ideal for gravel or rough urban surfaces. The tires are mounted on aluminum rims, which appear to be in good shape. Both wheels are secured with standard quick-release mechanisms.

Saddle: The saddle is a basic black vinyl model, showing slight wear but no visible damage. It's supported by a standard seat post with a quick-release mechanism for easy height adjustments.

Water Bottle & Holder: White water bottle that says 'Silvera for Seniors' is a sipper style 500 ml bottle and is on a metal bottle cage that is located in the vertical rod of the bike just above the front gear chain

Accessories:

Lock: A black coiled cable lock is attached around the frame, securing the bike when parked.
Green Bag: A small green bag is mounted on the handlebar stem, possibly used for small items or tools.
Reflectors: There are red rear reflectors and front-mounted white reflectors for visibility.
Bar Code: The bike features a barcode sticker on the frame near the handlebar with the number "70410 01847."

Distinctive Marks: The handlebar stem has visible rust and slight wear, while the grips show signs of extended use.

title: 2023 Cervélo ASPERO – 5 GRX RX815 Di2
description:
• Premium grade gravel race carbon GRX Di2/disc brake bicycle that improves on original design
• Upgraded carbon fiber, and lay-up schedule for stiffer, faster design with aerodynamic improvements
• Use of D-shaped, tapered carbon fork steerer design, with included bar & stem for full wire/housing integration including dropper post
• Change allows for more aero headtube design and reduced drag from integrated routing
• Lower, more stable road-like feel from overall geometry, chainstay length, BB drop
• Accepts 700c x 43mm tires / 650b x 51mm tires; minimum 4mm side clearance
• Trail Mixer fork dropouts can be switched for +/- 5mm selectable fork offset; 62mm optimal Trail
• Forward position=decreased trail=faster handling | Rearward position=increased trail=slower handling
• Squoval.Max tube designs, enlarged and enhanced for rough surfaces; fork/downtube integration
• Large downtube BBright bottom bracket shell w/ oversized dropped chainstays
• Carbon lay-up, round seatpost, larger volume tires contribute to rider comfort
• 1x/2x compatible, with mechanical or electronic shifting systems; removable braze-on FD mount
• Frameset
o Frame: Aspero-5 Carbon, full internal routing, Flat Mount caliper interface, 12/142mm TA dropout
o Fork: Tapered Áspero-5 Fork, Adjustable Trail; D-Shaped steerer, Flat Mount caliper interface, 12/100mm TA dropout
o Headset: FSA IS-2 1¼ x 1½"
o Thru-axles: Cervélo indexed/threaded: 12/100mm, 12/142mm
• Groupset: Shimano GRX 815 Di2 2x11 w/ hydraulic disc braking; internal eTube wiring, seatpost battery
o Dual Control levers: Di2 Shifting with mineral oil based hydraulic braking set-up
o Brake Calipers/Rotors: Shimano GRX RX815 Hydraulic Disc w/ SM-RT800 CL 160mm rotors
o Derailleurs: GRX 815 Di2 Front (braze-on), Rear (up to 34T max cog)
o Crankset/BB: GRX 810 48/31T / Cervélo BBright JY-BB 24 bottom bracket
o Cassette/Chain: HG800 11-34T / CN701
• Wheelset & Tires
o Cervélo Reserve with 32mm Carbon rims (IRW 24mm), TLR, DT350 HG11, CL disc
o Panaracer Gravel King SK TLR 700x38
• Cockpit & Accessories
o Handlebar: Carbon AB09 16° Flared Carbon, 31.8mm, integrated routing; Cervélo bar tape
o Stem: Cervélo ST32 Alloy, 31.8, (for Cervélo D-shaped fork steerer tube, integrated routing)
o Seatpost: Cervélo SP19 Carbon 27.2mm round (48-54cm Zero offset, 56-61cm, 25mm setback)
o Saddle: Prologo Dimension Nack
o Cervélo accessories: Stem Faceplate Computer/Acc mount, Rear Accessory Mount, and Smartpak 400A Top Tube Storage Bag, Downtube Protector
• Component specifics by frame size:
o 48cm Bar width: 38c-c, Stem length: 70mm, Crank length: 165mm
• Colors: Five Black
• Sizes: 48 cm
• Weight: 18.69 lbs (56cm)

Geometry
Size	48
Wheel Size	700
Stack	505mm
Reach	370mm
Head Angle	71°
Seat Angle	74.5°
Top Tube Length	512mm
Head Tube Length	83mm
Standover	690mm
Fork Trail	58.6mm
Fork Offset	52mm
Bottom Bracket Drop	78.5mm
Front Center	583mm
Wheelbase	990mm
Chainstay Length	420mm

title: 2024 Trek Marlin 5 - Gen 2
description:
Stolen Trek Marlin 5 – very distinctive color and setup, not a stock build.

Frame color: Volt Blue → Miami Green fade (bright, high-visibility gradient that really stands out)
Rear pannier rack/bag attached
Hybrid clip-in pedals (clip-in + flat combo)
Handlebar-mounted phone holder
Center/top tube bag mounted just behind the stem
Front and rear lights installed
Bell + horn on handlebars
Side-view mirrors on BOTH sides (uncommon and highly noticeable)

This bike is extremely recognizable due to the bright blue-to-green fade and the number of accessories. It looks more like a commuter/touring setup than a typical mountain bike.

If you see anything matching this description, even partially, please reach out.

description:
Brief description: Full suspension bike, 26” wheels, white with distinctive Purple brake handles, purple disc brake callipers, purple brake discs and golden wheel hubs. Marin “Bear” logo on front of bike and bar end caps.

Components: 
Forks: 		Fox Float 120 RL
Rear shock:	Fox RP3 - Kashima coated
Handlebars:	BrandX
Seat Post: 	BrandX dropper post
Brakes: 	Hope Tech E4 Callipers (in purple) with Tech 2 brake levers (in purple)
Disc rotors:	Hope 180mm front, 160mm rear (in purple)
Wheel hub:	Hope Evo 2 (in gold)
Wheel rims: 	26” Mavic XC717 Disc
Tires:

Green ‘Hope’ sticker on lower part of swing arm by rear gears. Black neoprene/velcro chain protector on lower part of swing arm - right hand side only.
The bike had significant upgrades from the stock purchase, notably the braking system was added as an upgrade, as were the wheels, dropper seat post, handlebar and front cog.

title: 1994 Specialized Crossroads
description:
I came outside this morning 12/7/24 to find my prized commuter had disappeared from my backyard. Near City Park and Mountain. Likely stolen in the night. It was a barn find that I rebuilt and added many non-stock parts parts to, and I haven't seen seen anything similar to it around town. Unfortunately I did not write down the serial number, but I will be filing reports with Bike Index and the city.

~1994 Red Specialized Crossroads in XL/Large with White Decals

UPANBIKE butterfly bars Wrapped in Red (Most Distinctive)
Black Avid Speed Dial Brake Levers
Koski V-Brake front/ Avid Single Digit Rear Calipers
No-name 3x8 Speed Shifters
Shimano Deore LX Rear Derailleur
Vintage Shimano Exage? Front Derailleur
Puroma Red Pedals
Black Easton Front Wheel - Specialized Sawtooth Tire
Dura-Ace Hub With Mavic Rim Rear Wheel - Goodyear Transit Speed Tire
Silver Sunrace 11-40t Cassette on the Rear Wheel (Big Gear on back wheel)
Bontrager Seat

Other Stuff:

Faded Brown REI Flash Pannier on left side
Jim Blackburn Bottle Cage and JBlackburn Rear Rack
Adjustable Bottle Cage on seat tube
RockBros Tubetop Bag and Frame Bag
Polar Colorado Water Bottle
Red Phone Mount
Blitzu Bike Light (Paint Heavily Faded)
OnGuard U Lock (Unlocked)

title: Supercycle BREEZE
description:
Color: Dark blue frame with a glossy finish. The paint shows minor scuffs and wear, particularly near the bottom bracket and chainstay, reflecting typical usage.

Frame Size & Design: The bike features a medium-sized frame with a slightly slanted top tube. The tube joints are visibly welded and reinforced, providing extra durability for off-road or urban riding.

Gears & Drivetrain: Equipped with a standard multi-speed gear setup. It has a triple chainring in the front and a 7-speed cassette in the rear, allowing for smooth shifting over a range of terrains. The derailleurs and shifters are operational but show minor signs of use.

Brakes: The bike uses traditional rim brakes, both front and rear, which are in good working order. The brake levers on the handlebars are black and slightly worn from use.

Handlebars: The handlebars are flat, straight, and black, with wear visible on the grips. The right grip end has a small cap missing, exposing the inner handlebar tube. The handlebar stem shows slight rust and wear, while the cables for the brakes and shifters are externally routed, curving smoothly toward the frame.

Tires & Wheels: The tires are thick, knobby, and designed for multi-surface riding, with a deep tread pattern ideal for gravel or rough urban surfaces. The tires are mounted on aluminum rims, which appear to be in good shape. Both wheels are secured with standard quick-release mechanisms.

Saddle: The saddle is a basic black vinyl model, showing slight wear but no visible damage. It's supported by a standard seat post with a quick-release mechanism for easy height adjustments.

Water Bottle & Holder: White water bottle that says 'Silvera for Seniors' is a sipper style 500 ml bottle and is on a metal bottle cage that is located in the vertical rod of the bike just above the front gear chain

Accessories:

Lock: A black coiled cable lock is attached around the frame, securing the bike when parked.
Green Bag: A small green bag is mounted on the handlebar stem, possibly used for small items or tools.
Reflectors: There are red rear reflectors and front-mounted white reflectors for visibility.
Bar Code: The bike features a barcode sticker on the frame near the handlebar with the number "70410 01847."

Distinctive Marks: The handlebar stem has visible rust and slight wear, while the grips show signs of extended use.

title: GT Bicycles Transeo 4.0
description:
The GT Transeo 4.0 is a hybrid bike designed for riders who want something versatile — comfortable for commuting and fitness rides, but also capable of handling light trails and gravel. Here are the main details about it:
Overview
Category: Hybrid / Fitness / Commuter
Brand: GT Bicycles 
Frame: Aluminum (lightweight, durable, upright geometry)
Fork: Equipped with a front suspension fork with a lockout option for efficient road riding
Wheels: 700c wheels (like a road bike, but wider tires for stability and comfort)
Speeds/Drivetrain: Typically a 3x8 (24-speed) Shimano or microSHIFT drivetrain
Brakes: Mechanical disc brakes (reliable stopping power in all weather, but not hydraulic)
Tires: 700x40c (good balance of speed and shock absorption)
Other features: Rack and fender mounts, blue MEC carrying panniers.

title: Supercycle BREEZE
description:
Color: Dark blue frame with a glossy finish. The paint shows minor scuffs and wear, particularly near the bottom bracket and chainstay, reflecting typical usage.

Frame Size & Design: The bike features a medium-sized frame with a slightly slanted top tube. The tube joints are visibly welded and reinforced, providing extra durability for off-road or urban riding.

Gears & Drivetrain: Equipped with a standard multi-speed gear setup. It has a triple chainring in the front and a 7-speed cassette in the rear, allowing for smooth shifting over a range of terrains. The derailleurs and shifters are operational but show minor signs of use.

Brakes: The bike uses traditional rim brakes, both front and rear, which are in good working order. The brake levers on the handlebars are black and slightly worn from use.

Handlebars: The handlebars are flat, straight, and black, with wear visible on the grips. The right grip end has a small cap missing, exposing the inner handlebar tube. The handlebar stem shows slight rust and wear, while the cables for the brakes and shifters are externally routed, curving smoothly toward the frame.

Tires & Wheels: The tires are thick, knobby, and designed for multi-surface riding, with a deep tread pattern ideal for gravel or rough urban surfaces. The tires are mounted on aluminum rims, which appear to be in good shape. Both wheels are secured with standard quick-release mechanisms.

Saddle: The saddle is a basic black vinyl model, showing slight wear but no visible damage. It's supported by a standard seat post with a quick-release mechanism for easy height adjustments.

Water Bottle & Holder: White water bottle that says 'Silvera for Seniors' is a sipper style 500 ml bottle and is on a metal bottle cage that is located in the vertical rod of the bike just above the front gear chain

Accessories:

Lock: A black coiled cable lock is attached around the frame, securing the bike when parked.
Green Bag: A small green bag is mounted on the handlebar stem, possibly used for small items or tools.
Reflectors: There are red rear reflectors and front-mounted white reflectors for visibility.
Bar Code: The bike features a barcode sticker on the frame near the handlebar with the number "70410 01847."

Distinctive Marks: The handlebar stem has visible rust and slight wear, while the grips show signs of extended use.

title: 2023 Cervélo ASPERO – 5 GRX RX815 Di2
description:
• Premium grade gravel race carbon GRX Di2/disc brake bicycle that improves on original design
• Upgraded carbon fiber, and lay-up schedule for stiffer, faster design with aerodynamic improvements
• Use of D-shaped, tapered carbon fork steerer design, with included bar & stem for full wire/housing integration including dropper post
• Change allows for more aero headtube design and reduced drag from integrated routing
• Lower, more stable road-like feel from overall geometry, chainstay length, BB drop
• Accepts 700c x 43mm tires / 650b x 51mm tires; minimum 4mm side clearance
• Trail Mixer fork dropouts can be switched for +/- 5mm selectable fork offset; 62mm optimal Trail
• Forward position=decreased trail=faster handling | Rearward position=increased trail=slower handling
• Squoval.Max tube designs, enlarged and enhanced for rough surfaces; fork/downtube integration
• Large downtube BBright bottom bracket shell w/ oversized dropped chainstays
• Carbon lay-up, round seatpost, larger volume tires contribute to rider comfort
• 1x/2x compatible, with mechanical or electronic shifting systems; removable braze-on FD mount
• Frameset
o Frame: Aspero-5 Carbon, full internal routing, Flat Mount caliper interface, 12/142mm TA dropout
o Fork: Tapered Áspero-5 Fork, Adjustable Trail; D-Shaped steerer, Flat Mount caliper interface, 12/100mm TA dropout
o Headset: FSA IS-2 1¼ x 1½"
o Thru-axles: Cervélo indexed/threaded: 12/100mm, 12/142mm
• Groupset: Shimano GRX 815 Di2 2x11 w/ hydraulic disc braking; internal eTube wiring, seatpost battery
o Dual Control levers: Di2 Shifting with mineral oil based hydraulic braking set-up
o Brake Calipers/Rotors: Shimano GRX RX815 Hydraulic Disc w/ SM-RT800 CL 160mm rotors
o Derailleurs: GRX 815 Di2 Front (braze-on), Rear (up to 34T max cog)
o Crankset/BB: GRX 810 48/31T / Cervélo BBright JY-BB 24 bottom bracket
o Cassette/Chain: HG800 11-34T / CN701
• Wheelset & Tires
o Cervélo Reserve with 32mm Carbon rims (IRW 24mm), TLR, DT350 HG11, CL disc
o Panaracer Gravel King SK TLR 700x38
• Cockpit & Accessories
o Handlebar: Carbon AB09 16° Flared Carbon, 31.8mm, integrated routing; Cervélo bar tape
o Stem: Cervélo ST32 Alloy, 31.8, (for Cervélo D-shaped fork steerer tube, integrated routing)
o Seatpost: Cervélo SP19 Carbon 27.2mm round (48-54cm Zero offset, 56-61cm, 25mm setback)
o Saddle: Prologo Dimension Nack
o Cervélo accessories: Stem Faceplate Computer/Acc mount, Rear Accessory Mount, and Smartpak 400A Top Tube Storage Bag, Downtube Protector
• Component specifics by frame size:
o 48cm Bar width: 38c-c, Stem length: 70mm, Crank length: 165mm
• Colors: Five Black
• Sizes: 48 cm
• Weight: 18.69 lbs (56cm)

Geometry
Size	48
Wheel Size	700
Stack	505mm
Reach	370mm
Head Angle	71°
Seat Angle	74.5°
Top Tube Length	512mm
Head Tube Length	83mm
Standover	690mm
Fork Trail	58.6mm
Fork Offset	52mm
Bottom Bracket Drop	78.5mm
Front Center	583mm
Wheelbase	990mm
Chainstay Length	420mm

title: 2014 Bianchi Volpe
description: When we introduced our first cyclocross bike to America in 1986, few riders were even into cyclocross racing. Despite that, it didn't take long for cycling enthusiasts to figure out what our new Volpe excelled at; everything! Long distance road rides. Gravel road excursions. City commuting. Loaded touring. Rail trail adventures. Neighborhood errands. The Volpe checks off all the boxes! Short of criterium racing and technical single track, the Volpe handles all types of riding efficiently and comfortably. The Volpe melds the responsive handling of a road bike with the durability of a mountain bike. A variety of surfaces and conditions can be conquered thanks to greater tire clearance and grime-shedding cantilever brakes. Our exclusive Chromolite frame and steel fork provide a smooth ride and the strength to carry whatever supplies your adventure calls for. With eyelets for front and rear racks and a set of fenders, the Volpe is one of the most versatile models we offer. Frame: Bianchi Cr-Mo, triple butted main tubes, tig welded..

title: 2020 Jamis Hudson Step-Thru
description:
Think of our Hudson as part Explorer and part Earth Cruiser. Capable but cool. Casual but on it. We blended the flowing curves and shaped tubes of seemingly disparate platforms like our carbon Dakota 29’ers and our aluminum DXT’s and came up with a smart frame design that rides comfortably, casually and efficiently while making a bold statement all at the same time.

Hudson Lite
When it comes to recreational riding on a so-called comfort bike, nothing gets more uncomfortable faster than having to pedal more weight than necessary. Which is why the Hudson Sport is built with lightweight 6061 aluminum tubing. We design comfort into the naturally stiffer ride of the aluminum tubes by specifying large volume tires and lengthening the chainstays and wheelbase for a stretch-limo sort of ride.

Easy Rider
Specially designed for flat-footed starts and stops. With your weight evenly distributed over the crank for comfortable and efficient pedaling on the saddle or off. The Hudson’s laid back seat tube and lowered bottom bracket height allows riders to position their saddles for efficient leg extension and still easily reach the ground while seated for balance when stopped. The longer chainstays and wheelbase in tandem with the lower center of gravity offers a very smooth, stable ride.

Step Right In
Our step-through version is exceptionally easy to mount thanks to the low BB DROP and super-low position of the S-bent top tube. No more swinging a leg over the rear wheel, or pulling a knee up to the chest to step over the top tube to mount a bike.

Get Fit
All frame sizes offer an extra-long head tube and hi- rise handlebars for a comfortable, upright ride position.

Where's The Other Brake Lever?
The SureStop braking system simplifies stopping by requiring only one brake lever to operate both front & rear brakes. It makes riding safer too by reducing the risk of front wheel lock-up. SureStop uses the momentum of the rear wheel to actively modulate the front brake, enabling smooth, controlled stopping. With only one hand!

Best In Class Comfort
Our super-plush saddle features Memory Foam padding for optimum support with maximum comfort. The slip-resistant suede-like top prevents sliding and the burnished side panels reduce leg friction while pedaling. Elastomer springs also help to soak up road vibrations. Offered in gender-specific sizes.

Pump Up The Volume
Our Hudson tires are ready to roll, coming in a tall 27.5” diameter with a bump-chewing 2.35” width. The big benefit of that footprint is larger air volume in the tire and tube, which results in lower air pressure for a more comfortable ride than tires with smaller volumes and higher air pressures.

Clean And Simple
The Hudson’s alloy crankset features an outer-ring chainguard, with another full-length chainguard running the length of the chain from seatstay to chainring. So socks, slacks, skirts and dresses are well-protected from the chain.

- 6061 TIG-welded aluminum frame
- Hi-tensile unicrown fork
- Alex DC25 alloy double-wall wheel set
- 7-Speed Shimano Altus components

Our Hudson tires are ready to roll, coming in a tall 27.5” diameter with a bump-chewing 2.35” width. The big benefit of that footprint is larger air volume in the tire and tube, which results in lower air pressure for a more comfortable ride than tires with smaller volumes and higher air pressures.

Clean And Simple
The Hudson’s alloy crankset features an outer-ring chainguard, with another full-length chainguard running the length of the chain from seatstay to chainring. So socks, slacks, skirts and dresses are well-protected from the chain.

- 6061 TIG-welded aluminum frame
- Hi-tensile unicrown fork
- Alex DC25 alloy double-wall wheel set
- 7-Speed Shimano Altus components

title: 2009 Breezer Uptown 8
description: Year 2009 black "Breezer Uptown 8" bike stolen during afternoon. Locked at bike rack (with a U-lock) at corner of 8th and Kelly, next to building entrance.  Bike, lock and helmet were all gone when I arrived at 6 pm after work (last seen at 1 pm, securely locked). The bike looks like this: http://www.ecovelo.info/2009/09/20/road-test-breezer-uptown-8/It's a diamond-frame bike with an 8-speed internal gear hub, front dynamo with lights, and a full chaincase and rear rack. Also attached were a Wald folding wire basket (on the right side of the rack), a Pletscher folding kickstand, and a gray bar over the top of the top tube, for mounting a WeeRide kid's seat. The rear brake was replaced with a rollerbrake. Handlebars, seatpost and brake levers are non-stock. The saddle was a black Velo Orange leather saddle, covered with a rain cover. The rear wheel was locked with a frame-mounted "O" lock (ABUS brand). There are several distinctive scratches, including a mark on the right seat tube from where the kids' trailer attaches. I have the original receipt and many photos with the bike. This was my main form of transportation for 2 years.

title: 2024 Trek Marlin 5 - Gen 2
description:
Stolen Trek Marlin 5 – very distinctive color and setup, not a stock build.

Frame color: Volt Blue → Miami Green fade (bright, high-visibility gradient that really stands out)
Rear pannier rack/bag attached
Hybrid clip-in pedals (clip-in + flat combo)
Handlebar-mounted phone holder
Center/top tube bag mounted just behind the stem
Front and rear lights installed
Bell + horn on handlebars
Side-view mirrors on BOTH sides (uncommon and highly noticeable)

This bike is extremely recognizable due to the bright blue-to-green fade and the number of accessories. It looks more like a commuter/touring setup than a typical mountain bike.

If you see anything matching this description, even partially, please reach out.

title: 2010 Trek Gary Fisher
description:
Post for seat pole damaged. 
A man my age was yelling at it with the seat off, and I asled him what was wrong. He said the seat wouldn't tighten. I observed the frame damage and figured I could make it at least RIDEABLE for him. We walk it back to my apartment, and he starts getting all weirded out, as I'm working on it. He says he better leave, and I say that it's literally rideable until he can find a repair shop. He says "It's your bike now." 
Which leads me to believe it's stolen. I called Eugene, OR PD, and the serial numbers I gave them weren't showing up anywhere.
